threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the 'tube' instead of just 'the train,' and your bag's not filled with snacks for the wait at Dalston Junction.
Caught a whiff of fried chicken from the bus stop near the market, but it was just an old takeaway bag blowing in the wind. Classic Peckham.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a toddler sharing a Berliner Weisse with their Opa at the café on Weserstraße—now that's Neukölln living.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Neukölln will you find a bar serving cocktails in old shampoo bottles. It's bizarre and oddly charming. A true local touch.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they're local when they know to avoid BART during rush hour like the plague—especially after all those delays lately. Classic San Francisco move.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 19 during rush hour yesterday—someone was doing a full karaoke set. You could tell half the riders were utterly into it while the other half just rolled their eyes.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think 'tapas' is just a snack. In Gràcia, it’s a full-on meal—locals go for the whole experience, not just a plate or two.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by how they still look amazed at the processions during La Mercè, snapping selfies like it’s a rare street art piece.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still check the tube app obsessively, not realizing locals just use the bus or walk. It’s a whole different vibe when you stop planning every trip.
Last night on the 35, a guy in a bowler hat sang the whole way to Clapham. No one joined in, but we were all smirking. Only in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Watching the 104 bus driver give free rides to a group of kids on their way to school, while the usual stoppages seemed to just vanish. That's Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find techno parties in abandoned factories, not just for dancing but also for art installations that explode on weekends.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their reaction to a BART delay—actual frustration, not just a sigh. It's the difference between a missed meeting and a life interruption.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Witnessed a guy juggling for tips at the Van Ness stop while a cat in a backpack stared him down. Classic San Francisco moment right there.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think we all love vermut at any hour. But locals know the real vibe happens after 5 p.m., with a simple canya and some olives.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their enthusiasm for speaking Catalan, even when everyone else is comfortably slipping into Spanish.
